excerpt: When the seven-thousand-year cycle of Kashf (the period of revelation and disclosure) reaches completion, there gather at the horizon of Ashir—the tenth intellect—a total of one hundred twenty-four thousand forms, which the Qa'im receives and consolidates into the temple structure of the seventh thousand-year period.

As the period of revelation reaches its perfection and the cosmic book achieves its fullness, deterioration creeps in and multiplies. The Imams withdraw behind the veil (taqiyyah, prudential concealment), and knowledge of the esoteric realities gives way to knowledge of exoteric forms.
In the Age of Revelation (Kashf), there is no place for the Trustees of the Hidden (al-Mustawadeen); only the stations of the Firm Ones (al-Mustaqeen) obtain.
source_location_hint: ranks and stations in cycles of disclosure

excerpt: Our Master Ali declared: 'Every ten thousand years there appears a perfect form, and I am contained within that form.' In other words, he had gathered the seven thousand years of the veiled period and the three thousand years of the Fitra period as a unified whole.
source_location_hint: Ali's station as gatherer of forms

excerpt: Tasbih and Taqddis means attaining the knowledge of the Imam (a.s.). After the death of a believer, the account of action ceases, because the action is related to the body. But his passion for knowledge remains, which is why he is provided with knowledge in the Temple.
source_work: Kitab al-Yanabiʿ / al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama
source_authority: Ismaili philosophical corpus on gnosis and hierarchies
source_location_hint: Discussions of post-mortem knowledge acquisition in sacred precincts

excerpt: Subhan illa Allaah is the word of tanziyyah, which acknowledges the subsistence of Allah, and its purpose is to purify Him from the attributes of all creation. Sabah means to float. The force of the water does not allow the swimmer to come downwards, but keeps him upwards.
source_authority: Key text on divine transcendence and metaphysical principles
source_location_hint: Discussions of Tanzih and the preservation of divine greatness

excerpt: The son of every Imam (the one who is to become the Imam himself) is a prophet and is the opposite of praise for the believers. That is, praise be to Allah, Allah is the present Imam and Hamd is his successor son.
source_authority: Exposition of Imamate succession and prophetic ranks
source_location_hint: Sections on the designation and preparation of successors

excerpt: In the terminology of Da'wah, 'Hawl' is the inner chapter which collects the images of the gentlemen, and 'strength' is the chapter of the apparent which collects the chapters of the islands. This power is available only to the Imam, who is the Khalifa and Hijab of Ain Azimah.
source_authority: Esoteric doctrine on the twofold structure of the daʿwa
source_location_hint: Expositions on ḥawl (inner capacity) and quwwa (outer strength) in the hierarchy

excerpt: God has established a perfect balance. There are 9 parts of this verse and the number 9 indicates the extremity of the unit, its perfection and its essence, just as the city of Ramadan is the ninth month of the year and the summation of all the other months.
source_authority: Jurisprudential and esoteric exegesis of Quranic verses
source_location_hint: Numerological and structural analysis of Āyat al-Kursī
